headline: Apple’s stunning Nvidia flip reveals where AI money is moving
summary: Apple (AAPL) briefly overtook Nvidia (NVDA) as the world’s most valuable company during trading on July 17. However, Apple could not maintain the lead for long. Nvidia’s market capitalization was nearly $4.95 trillion compared with about $4.91 trillion for Apple when the markets closed on Friday. ...

headline: Bezos Backs AI Startup to Discover New Materials for Chipmaking
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Producing the world’s most in-demand semiconductors requires enormous amounts of energy and access to rare minerals. CuspAI, a two-year-old British startup, has raised nearly half a billion dollars on its bet that artificial intelligence can improve that process. headline: China vs. US AI: Moonshot's Kimi K3 may not be a total game changer quite yet
summary: Chinese artificial intelligence startup Moonshot made waves last week after showcasing its new Kimi K3 AI model, which is being hailed as the world's largest publicly available large language model available for download. Investors speculated whether this reveal was another DeepSeek moment for the AI landscape. Council on Foreign Relations (CFR) senior fellow for China and emerging technologies, Chris McGuire, speaks with Julie Hyman about the tightening AI race between China and the US and what these low-cost models from China represent for the global AI build-out.

content.title: China vs. US AI: Moonshot's Kimi K3 may not be a total game changer quite yet
content.description:
Chinese artificial intelligence startup Moonshot made waves last week after showcasing its new Kimi K3 AI model, which is being hailed as the world's largest publicly available large language model available for download. Investors speculated whether this reveal was another DeepSeek moment for the AI landscape.

Council on Foreign Relations (CFR) senior fellow for China and emerging technologies, Chris McGuire, speaks with Julie Hyman about the tightening AI race between China and the US and what these low-cost models from China represent for the global AI build-out.
content.summary: Chinese artificial intelligence startup Moonshot made waves last week after showcasing its new Kimi K3 AI model, which is being hailed as the world's largest publicly available large language model available for download. Investors speculated whether this reveal was another DeepSeek moment for the AI landscape. Council on Foreign Relations (CFR) senior fellow for China and emerging technologies, Chris McGuire, speaks with Julie Hyman about the tightening AI race between China and the US and what these low-cost models from China represent for the global AI build-out.

headline: Nvidia Stock Steadies, But It Needs Help for Real Gains
summary: Nvidia  stock has steadied above $200 a share, but could do with Big Tech companies reaffirming their commitments to spending big on its artificial-intelligence processors to spark a rally.  Nvidia shares rose 1.4% to $205.71 Monday.  Nvidia stock closed down 2.2% on Friday and only narrowly fended off being replaced by  Apple  as the world’s largest company by market valuation.

headline: BofA expects Apple to top analyst expectations on July 30
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America reiterated a Buy rating on Apple with a price target of $380 per share in a note on Monday, expecting the company to beat consensus in its fiscal third-quarter results, which will mark Tim Cook's last earnings call as CEO.

headline: Magnificent Seven Dominate S&P 500 Earnings, But That's Changing Dramatically
summary: The Magnificent Seven will again reign supreme over S&P 500 earnings, but their dominance is fading.  As second-quarter earnings reports start to flow in, analysts expect the Magnificent Seven companies to grow earnings by a combined 31.1%.  The other 493 S&P 500 companies are expected to increase earnings 22.8%, FactSet Senior Earnings Analyst John Butters wrote in a report.

content.title: Magnificent Seven Dominate S&P 500 Earnings, But That's Changing Dramatically
content.summary: The Magnificent Seven will again reign supreme over S&P 500 earnings, but their dominance is fading.  As second-quarter earnings reports start to flow in, analysts expect the Magnificent Seven companies to grow earnings by a combined 31.1%.  The other 493 S&P 500 companies are expected to increase earnings 22.8%, FactSet Senior Earnings Analyst John Butters wrote in a report.
